sutta: the buddha said monks being wise and mindful may become immeasurable concentration for in those who do verily in each 1 of you there shall arise a fivefold knowledge what fivefold knowledge 1 this concentration is verily a present happiness and a source of happiness for the future even in each this knowledge arises secondly this concentration is aryan not carnal or of the flesh even in each this knowledge arises thirdly this concentration is not the practice of base men or lower men even in this in each this con this knowledge arises fourth this concentration is the peace the excellent thing the winning of calm the attaining of 1 pointedness and the restraint that prevails is not a conscious restraint even this even in each this knowledge arises and fifth mindfully i barely enter upon this concentration mindfully i verily emerge from this concentration even in each this knowledge arises monks being wise and mindful make become immeasurable concentration for in each there shall arise this 5 own knowledge is the end of the suta
commentary: this is another suta where the buddha stresses home the point and that concentration is very important and these are the 5 advantages and the buddha said if you develop a measurable concentration firstly you attain happiness at the present moment and it also becomes a source of happiness for you in the future why because if you if you remember 1 of the sutas i mentioned where the buddha praised the sangha of monks he said there are monks in this sangha who have attained concentration and because of that they are likely to be reborn in the deva plane so by attaining concentration they have a very good chance of being born into the deva plane that is why it's a source of happiness for the future secondly this concentration is aryan it is not carnal this concentration has got to do with the aryan path don't look down on concentration it is an aryan it is a practice of the aryans without concentration the buddha said that you can't attain the aryan path and the third 1 this concentration is not the practice of basement not the practice of low men so here again it is it is similar to the previous statement that concentration is aryan if people worldly people cannot attain this this type of concentration and that is because the buddha said that the jhanas is 1 of the uttari manosa dhamma uttari manosa means higher than human a dhamma which is higher than human sometimes we translate this as supernormal attainment uh that means ordinary people can't attain it you have to you have to arise you have to rise above the worldly level to attain this concentration and then fourthly concept this concentration is the peace the excellent thing the winning of calm the attaining of 1 pointedness and the restraint that prevails is not a conscious restraint and this is a very important statement the restraint that prevails is not a conscious restraint what is this referring to this is probably referring to the restraint of the 5 hindrances the 5 hindrances when you attain concentration the 5 hindrances are automatically restrained and it is not a conscious restraint you don't have to make a conscious effort for example to curb your anger because anger is 1 of the hindrances you will is 1 of the it is the second hindrance out of the 5 hindrances but when you attain concentration that restraint is automatic you don't have to make an effort to be mindful to restrain your anger it automatically becomes restrained and the same essential desire don't have to make a conscious effort to restrain your sensual desire the mind is so calm it's not easy to stir calm mind so that is why this uh restraint is not a conscious restraint and the other other hindrances are sloth and topper restlessness and worry and doubt all these are automatically restrained and it is not a conscious restraint comes automatically with concentration that is why the buddha always stress on concentration and the fifth 1 also on the buddha says mindfully i enter upon this concentration mindfully i emerge from this concentration concentration the state of ghana is always to do with mindfulness it is a very intense state of mindfulness without an intense state of mindfulness you cannot attain the jaanas so always remember that don't don't believe that jana is a state of unmindfulness it is definitely not a state of mindfulness here is very clear mindfully i enter upon this concentration mindfully i emerge from this concentration so these are the 5 things now the nexus is
